content.problemStatement:
Awesome! - We Have Completed Our Word Counter Project.

Now that we have successfully implemented the Word Counter, here are some additional tasks you can try out:
- Explore Advanced Regular Expressions: We use the regular expression `\\b\\w+\\b` to extract words from a given text, it has some limitations - Doesn't Handle Contractions and hyphenated words Properly, explore more advanced regular expressions to overcome these limitations.
- Count the Number of Sentences: Count the number of sentences based on punctuation marks (., !, ?).
- Count the Number of Paragraphs:  Identify and count paragraphs based on line breaks.
- Identify the Most Frequent Word:  Display the word that appears the most in the text.
constraints:
- $1 \leq T \leq 100$
- $1 \leq N \leq 100$
- $1 \leq M \leq N\cdot(N-1)/2$
- $1 \leq u_i, v_i \leq N$
- $u_i \neq v_i$ for each $1 \leq i \leq M$.
- The sum of $N$ over all test cases won't exceed $100$.
content.inputFormat:
- The first line of input will contain a single integer $T$, denoting the number of test cases.
- Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.
    - The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers $N$ and $M$ — the number of vertices and edges of the graph, respectively.
    - The next $M$ lines describe the edges. The $i$-th of these $M$ lines contains two space-separated integers $u_i$ and $v_i$, denoting an edge between $u_i$ and $v_i$.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a new line the number of good subgraphs of $G$, modulo $10^9 + 7$.
